{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Executive Assistant to the Managing Director",
  "description": "Tivana Workforce is a workforce solutions company that provides services that unlock the full potential of human capital to drive business excellence and growth in an ever-evolving marketplace. Our Services include Staffing and Recruitment, Workforce Management, Training and Development, Technology Solutions, HR Consulting, Team Retreat Planning and Specialized services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant to the Managing Director

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Executive Assistant to the Managing Director provides high-level operational, administrative, and communication support to ensure the efficient functioning of the MD&rsquo;s office.
	The role requires strong organizational skills, discretion, business acumen, and the ability to coordinate across departments in a fast-paced real estate environment.
	The Executive Assistant will manage executive schedules, oversee administrative processes, support operational follow-through on strategic initiatives, and serve as a key communication liaison between the MD and internal/external stakeholders.


Key Responsibilities
Executive Support &amp; Administration:


	Manage the MD&rsquo;s calendar, appointments, meetings, and travel arrangements.
	Prepare meeting agendas, presentations, reports, and briefing documents.
	Take accurate minutes during meetings and track action items to completion.
	Screen calls, emails, and correspondence; prioritize and respond where appropriate.
	Maintain organized filing systems (digital and physical).


Operations Coordination:


	Track operational projects across departments (Sales, Marketing, Projects, Finance, Facility Management).
	Follow up with department heads on deliverables and performance targets.
	Monitor progress of real estate developments and prepare status reports for the MD.
	Assist in coordinating vendor relationships and contractor communications.
	Support implementation of strategic initiatives and operational improvements.


Communication &amp; Stakeholder Management:


	Serve as a primary liaison between the MD and internal teams.
	Draft professional correspondence, internal memos, and external communications.
	Coordinate meetings with investors, clients, government agencies, and partners.
	Maintain confidentiality in handling sensitive company information.
	Ensure timely communication of executive directives across the organization.


Documentation &amp; Reporting:


	Prepare executive dashboards and performance summaries.
	Compile data for board meetings and management reviews.
	Maintain contracts, agreements, and key corporate documents.
	Support documentation for regulatory and compliance requirements.


Event &amp; Meeting Coordination:


	Coordinate executive meetings, board sessions, and strategic retreats.
	Manage logistics for property site visits and high-level client meetings.
	Support corporate events, launches, and stakeholder engagements.


Office Administration Oversight:


	Supervise administrative staff (if applicable).
	Ensure smooth day-to-day functioning of the executive office.
	Implement efficient administrative systems and processes.
	Manage office supplies and support executive procurement needs.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Management, Communications, or related field.
	5+ years of experience as an Executive Assistant or in a senior administrative role.
	Experience in real estate, property development, construction, or related industry is an advantage.
	Strong knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook).


Core Competencies:


	Exceptional organizational and time-management skills
	High level of professionalism and discretion
	Strong written and verbal communication skills
	Strategic thinking and problem-solving ability
	Ability to multitask and work under pressure
	Attention to detail and accuracy
	Stakeholder management and relationship-building skills.


Personal Attributes:


	Proactive and solution-oriented
	High integrity and confidentiality
	Executive presence and professionalism
	Strong business judgment
	Calm under pressure.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV and Photograph to:&nbsp;tivanaworkforce@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-03",
  "validThrough": "2026-03-14",

Executive Assistant to the Managing Director Job at Tivana Workforce




        
    

        
        Tivana Workforce is a workforce solutions company that provides services that unlock the full potential of human capital to drive business excellence and growth in an ever-evolving marketplace. Our Services include Staffing and Recruitment, Workforce Management, Training and Development, Technology Solutions, HR Consulting, Team Retreat Planning and Specialized services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant to the Managing Director

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Executive Assistant to the Managing Director provides high-level operational, administrative, and communication support to ensure the efficient functioning of the MD’s office.
	The role requires strong organizational skills, discretion, business acumen, and the ability to coordinate across departments in a fast-paced real estate environment.
	The Executive Assistant will manage executive schedules, oversee administrative processes, support operational follow-through on strategic initiatives, and serve as a key communication liaison between the MD and internal/external stakeholders.


Key Responsibilities
Executive Support & Administration:


	Manage the MD’s calendar, appointments, meetings, and travel arrangements.
	Prepare meeting agendas, presentations, reports, and briefing documents.
	Take accurate minutes during meetings and track action items to completion.
	Screen calls, emails, and correspondence; prioritize and respond where appropriate.
	Maintain organized filing systems (digital and physical).


Operations Coordination:


	Track operational projects across departments (Sales, Marketing, Projects, Finance, Facility Management).
	Follow up with department heads on deliverables and performance targets.
	Monitor progress of real estate developments and prepare status reports for the MD.
	Assist in coordinating vendor relationships and contractor communications.
	Support implementation of strategic initiatives and operational improvements.


Communication & Stakeholder Management:


	Serve as a primary liaison between the MD and internal teams.
	Draft professional correspondence, internal memos, and external communications.
	Coordinate meetings with investors, clients, government agencies, and partners.
	Maintain confidentiality in handling sensitive company information.
	Ensure timely communication of executive directives across the organization.


Documentation & Reporting:


	Prepare executive dashboards and performance summaries.
	Compile data for board meetings and management reviews.
	Maintain contracts, agreements, and key corporate documents.
	Support documentation for regulatory and compliance requirements.


Event & Meeting Coordination:


	Coordinate executive meetings, board sessions, and strategic retreats.
	Manage logistics for property site visits and high-level client meetings.
	Support corporate events, launches, and stakeholder engagements.


Office Administration Oversight:


	Supervise administrative staff (if applicable).
	Ensure smooth day-to-day functioning of the executive office.
	Implement efficient administrative systems and processes.
	Manage office supplies and support executive procurement needs.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Management, Communications, or related field.
	5+ years of experience as an Executive Assistant or in a senior administrative role.
	Experience in real estate, property development, construction, or related industry is an advantage.
	Strong knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook).


Core Competencies:


	Exceptional organizational and time-management skills
	High level of professionalism and discretion
	Strong written and verbal communication skills
	Strategic thinking and problem-solving ability
	Ability to multitask and work under pressure
	Attention to detail and accuracy
	Stakeholder management and relationship-building skills.


Personal Attributes:


	Proactive and solution-oriented
	High integrity and confidentiality
	Executive presence and professionalism
	Strong business judgment
	Calm under pressure.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV and Photograph to: tivanaworkforce@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Biomedical&nbsp;is the first pharmaceutical company in Nigeria to manufacture and distribute intravenous fluids, Dialysis and irrigation fluids and oral rehydration salts we commenced production on August 21, 1981 at our factory located in Ilorin, Kwara State is spread over 24,380sqm.

As pioneer producers on this life very essential life-saving medication, we pride ourselves in producing high quality products. Our trail blazing efforts has made Nigeria self sufficient in intravenous and other perfusion fluids.

We seek applications from suitable, qualified and experienced candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Quality Control Manager / Head, Quality Control

Job Location:&nbsp;Ilorin, Kwara

The Role


	Proven experience in a leadership or managerial capacity
	In-depth knowledge of NAFDAC regulations
	Strong analytical, problem-solving, and project managerial skills
	Experience with advanced quality control software and tools.


Requirements


	A Bachelor&#39;s Degree in a relevant scientific field (e.g., Chemistry, Industrial chemistry, Biochemistry, Pure and Applied Chemistry with minimum of 2:1)
	15 years of experience in quality control roles.


Renumeration
N3,600,000 &ndash; N4,800,000 per annum.

Quality Control Manager / Head, Quality Control Job at Biomedical Limited




        
    

        
        Biomedical Limited was incorporated as a private limited liability company in April 1978, with interest in the manufacture of critical care products indispensable in healthcare delivery. Biomedical is the first pharmaceutical company in Nigeria to manufacture and distribute intravenous fluids, Dialysis and irrigation fluids and oral rehydration salts we commenced production on August 21, 1981 at our factory located in Ilorin, Kwara State is spread over 24,380sqm.

As pioneer producers on this life very essential life-saving medication, we pride ourselves in producing high quality products. Our trail blazing efforts has made Nigeria self sufficient in intravenous and other perfusion fluids.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We seek applications from suitable, qualified and experienced candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control Manager / Head, Quality Control

Job Location: Ilorin, Kwara

The Role


	Proven experience in a leadership or managerial capacity
	In-depth knowledge of NAFDAC regulations
	Strong analytical, problem-solving, and project managerial skills
	Experience with advanced quality control software and tools.


Requirements


	A Bachelor's Degree in a relevant scientific field (e.g., Chemistry, Industrial chemistry, Biochemistry, Pure and Applied Chemistry with minimum of 2:1)
	15 years of experience in quality control roles.


Renumeration
N3,600,000 – N4,800,000 per annum.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: solaotitolaye@yahoo.com, recruitment@biomedicalng.com, and sola.otitolaye@biomedicalng.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Buyer / Procurement Expediter

Job Location:&nbsp;Port Harcourt, Rivers
Job type: Contract&nbsp;

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Buyer / Procurement Expediter to support project procurement activities by managing purchasing processes and expediting vendor deliveries to meet project schedules.
	The role requires strong coordination with suppliers, logistics providers, and internal stakeholders to ensure timely and compliant procurement of materials and services.


Key Responsibilities


	Execute purchase orders in line with approved requisitions, budgets, and procurement procedures.
	Expedite vendor deliveries to ensure materials and equipment meet project timelines.
	Follow up with suppliers on order status, production progress, inspections, and shipment schedules.
	Coordinate with logistics, warehouse, and project teams to ensure timely receipt of goods.
	Track procurement milestones and maintain accurate expediting and delivery reports.
	Resolve supplier delays, shortages, and non-conformances in collaboration with internal teams.
	Ensure procurement documentation (POs, delivery notes, certificates) is complete and compliant.
	Support vendor evaluation, performance tracking, and supplier relationship management.
	Liaise with finance on invoices, GRNs, and payment processing where required.


Required Qualification and Experience


	HND / B.Sc Degree in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Engineering, or related field.
	Must have 3&ndash;5 years of experience in buying, procurement, or expediting roles (EPC, oil &amp; gas, construction, or industrial projects preferred).
	Strong knowledge of procurement processes, expediting, and vendor coordination.
	Experience tracking deliveries, managing lead times, and resolving supply delays.
	Good understanding of logistics, inspection, and material receiving processes.
	Proficiency in MS Excel and procurement/ERP systems.
	Strong communication, follow-up, and stakeholder management skills.


Key Competencies:


	Procurement expediting &amp; follow-up
	Vendor coordination &amp; performance monitoring
	Schedule-driven execution
	Attention to detail &amp; documentation control
	Problem-solving and escalation management
	Time management &amp; accountability


Method of Application&nbsp;

Buyer / Procurement Expediter Job at Marconi




        
    

        
        Marconi is a service platform at disposal for all the players in the Oil & Gas and Renewable Energy industries. Designed and equipped to aggressively pursuing the wider market for both large and medium sized projects.?Marconi strategic focus is on delivering world-class services that adhere to international standards while full in compliance with local regulatory frameworks and fostering local content.?

Marconi is set to be an EPCI powerhouse and Logistic Base supporting Africa’s industries requirements with innovative approach and combined with strategic partnerships to serve diverse clientele – from small local businesses to global industry leaders?.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Position: Buyer / Procurement Expediter

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Job type: Contract 

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Buyer / Procurement Expediter to support project procurement activities by managing purchasing processes and expediting vendor deliveries to meet project schedules.
	The role requires strong coordination with suppliers, logistics providers, and internal stakeholders to ensure timely and compliant procurement of materials and services.


Key Responsibilities


	Execute purchase orders in line with approved requisitions, budgets, and procurement procedures.
	Expedite vendor deliveries to ensure materials and equipment meet project timelines.
	Follow up with suppliers on order status, production progress, inspections, and shipment schedules.
	Coordinate with logistics, warehouse, and project teams to ensure timely receipt of goods.
	Track procurement milestones and maintain accurate expediting and delivery reports.
	Resolve supplier delays, shortages, and non-conformances in collaboration with internal teams.
	Ensure procurement documentation (POs, delivery notes, certificates) is complete and compliant.
	Support vendor evaluation, performance tracking, and supplier relationship management.
	Liaise with finance on invoices, GRNs, and payment processing where required.


Required Qualification and Experience


	HND / B.Sc Degree in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Engineering, or related field.
	Must have 3–5 years of experience in buying, procurement, or expediting roles (EPC, oil & gas, construction, or industrial projects preferred).
	Strong knowledge of procurement processes, expediting, and vendor coordination.
	Experience tracking deliveries, managing lead times, and resolving supply delays.
	Good understanding of logistics, inspection, and material receiving processes.
	Proficiency in MS Excel and procurement/ERP systems.
	Strong communication, follow-up, and stakeholder management skills.


Key Competencies:


	Procurement expediting & follow-up
	Vendor coordination & performance monitoring
	Schedule-driven execution
	Attention to detail & documentation control
	Problem-solving and escalation management
	Time management & accountability


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@marconi.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Sales Executive",
  "description": "Vitafoam Nigeria Plc is Nigeria&#39;s leading manufacturer of flexible, reconstituted and rigid foam products. It has the largest foam manufacturing and distribution network which facilitates just-in-time delivery of its products throughout Nigeria. Incorporated on 4th August, 1962 and listed on the floor of the Nigerian Stock Exchange in 1978. Vitafoam&#39;s successful brands remain household names in the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Sales Executive&nbsp;

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	We are seeking high-performing Customer Sales Executives with proven experience in selling tangible goods to join our dynamic team invitafoam subsidiaries (VITABLOM).
	The ideal candidates will be responsible for driving sales growth, managing customer relationships, and delivering excellent service that converts prospects into loyal customers.


Key Responsibilities


	Actively engage in field or direct sales of physical/tangible products
	Identify and pursue new business opportunities
	Maintain and grow relationships with existing clients
	Achieve or exceed sales targets and KPIs
	Provide after-sales support and resolve customer complaints professionally
	Prepare and submit regular sales reports


Requirements


	A minimum of a B.Sc. / HND in Marketing, Business Administration, or any related field.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in sales of tangible goods (e.g., FMCG, electronics, furniture, industrial products, etc.)
	Strong negotiation, communication, and interpersonal skills
	Self-driven, target-oriented, and customer-focused
	Must be familiar with the local market terrain
	Ability to work independently and in a team.


Benefits


	Salary negotiable
	Pension
	HMO


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified&nbsp;candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;career.vitafoam@vitafoam.com.ng&nbsp;using &ldquo;Customer Sales Executive &ndash; Abuja&rdquo; as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-02",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Vitafoam Nigeria Plc"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }

Customer Sales Executive Job at Vitafoam Nigeria Plc




        
    

        
        Vitafoam Nigeria Plc is Nigeria's leading manufacturer of flexible, reconstituted and rigid foam products. It has the largest foam manufacturing and distribution network which facilitates just-in-time delivery of its products throughout Nigeria. Incorporated on 4th August, 1962 and listed on the floor of the Nigerian Stock Exchange in 1978. Vitafoam's successful brands remain household names in the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Sales Executive 

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking high-performing Customer Sales Executives with proven experience in selling tangible goods to join our dynamic team invitafoam subsidiaries (VITABLOM).
	The ideal candidates will be responsible for driving sales growth, managing customer relationships, and delivering excellent service that converts prospects into loyal customers.


Key Responsibilities


	Actively engage in field or direct sales of physical/tangible products
	Identify and pursue new business opportunities
	Maintain and grow relationships with existing clients
	Achieve or exceed sales targets and KPIs
	Provide after-sales support and resolve customer complaints professionally
	Prepare and submit regular sales reports


Requirements


	A minimum of a B.Sc. / HND in Marketing, Business Administration, or any related field.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in sales of tangible goods (e.g., FMCG, electronics, furniture, industrial products, etc.)
	Strong negotiation, communication, and interpersonal skills
	Self-driven, target-oriented, and customer-focused
	Must be familiar with the local market terrain
	Ability to work independently and in a team.


Benefits


	Salary negotiable
	Pension
	HMO


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: career.vitafoam@vitafoam.com.ng using “Customer Sales Executive – Abuja” as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
